observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Inventory Committee (i) complete the physical count of all properties of the
Municipality and prepare the RPCPPE; (ii) in coordination with the
Municipal Accountant, expedite reconciliation of the inventory reports with
59
the accounting records, taking into consideration the deficiencies noted in
audit; and (iii) submit the final copies thereof to the Audit Team;
b. GSO (i) prepare and submit the weekly RSPI for proper recording thereof in
the books; (ii) assign separate ICS control numbers for low-valued and high-
valued semi-expendable property to improve tracking and prevent
misclassification; (iii) maintain a complete and updated property cards and
LRNPCs to facilitate the reconciliation thereof with the accounting records;
and (iv) submit IIRUP with complete details and supporting documents in
accordance with the Manual on Disposal and Appraisal of Government
Properties;
c. Municipal Accountant (i) fast track the identification and reclassification
process by establishing a dedicated task force to expedite the assessment of
PPE items that qualify as semi-expendable properties; (ii) revise the
accounting policies and procedures to align with the increased capitalization
threshold from ₱15,000.00 to ₱50,000.00 in accordance with COA Circular
2024-006; (iii) determine the unidentified PPE for proper valuation and
allocation of its cost against its estimated useful life; (iv) reclassify the affected
PPE items, establish individual SLs for the 60 rooms of the Isolation Building
and other assets requiring separate PPE SLs and ensure that all PPE acquired
at lump sum prices are properly allocated based on the cost breakdown or
relative fair value; and (v) gather relevant information on the confiscated
properties and disclose it accordingly in the NTFS; and
d. Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate the registration of the confiscated
watercrafts under the name of the Municipality, if warranted.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, (i) submit to the
Audit Team all the supporting documents of the 26 undocumented parcels of
land; (ii) submit copies of the Deed of Absolute Sale/Donation and the status
of the proceedings instituted for the issuance of TCTs and TDs under the name
of the municipal government for the land totaling ₱11,700,137.76, for
validation; and (iii) furnish the Municipal Accountant with pertinent
documents of the unrecorded land as bases for its recognition in the books; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) update the real property ledger cards with complete
details/information for each real property acquired by the Municipality in
accordance with Section 15 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II; (ii) record and
update the Land account upon submission of the pertinent documents by the
GSO and Municipal Assessor; and (iii) disclose the reclamation project in the
NTFS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) based on available records,
instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Treasurer to identify the unimplemented projects used as source of the amount
placed in the time deposit account for their proper reporting and evaluation; (b)
once determined, instruct the MPDC, to immediately look into the status of those
unimplemented projects for their immediate implementation or proper
68
reprogramming, if implementation is already not feasible; and (c) refrain from
placing funds from unimplemented projects in time deposits.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer report the related continuing appropriations in the SAAOB and
SCBAA and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team for fair presentation of the
budgetary reports as at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. GSO to submit the RPCI to the Audit Team for evaluation and to the
Municipal Accountant for proper recording;
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all purchases of inventoriable items are
recorded upon receipt and that the SLC is properly maintained in accordance
with Sections 120, 121 and 122 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
c. GSO and Municipal Accountant coordinate accordingly in recording the
issuances/consumption of inventories on a monthly basis to ensure the timely
72
recognition of the related expenses in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO, the Municipal
Engineer, and the MPDC, (i) to conduct a comprehensive physical count of all
road infrastructure assets, including those located in Dinet and Maobanen
Islands; (ii) prepare and submit the RPCLRN; and (iii) ensure the inclusion of
essential details such as road names, road IDs, exact locations, lengths, types,
and condition of each road segment in the road map, as required by COA
Circular No. 2015-018;
b. Require the GSO to prepare and submit the RLRN at the end of the
accounting period, as required under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008,
ensuring the report includes comprehensive details such as road ID/PIN, road
name, components, descriptions, and the corresponding costs;
c. Require the Municipal Engineer to (i) submit a complete report on the costs,
description and specifications and cost segregation of the road components to
the Municipal Accountant as bases for disclosures in the NTFS; and (ii)
coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office and the MPDC to provide the
75
complete information, specification, and cost breakdown of all road
components, including for donated lots/ROWs such as the Quilala access road;
and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant (i) to reconcile the RPCLRN and RLRN
with the SLs upon receipt to ensure accurate financial reporting; (ii) record
the donated road lots based on the complete report from the Municipal
Engineer; (iii) with the help of the Municipal Engineer, fully identify the
MPPs not intended for vehicular use, and reclassify to its appropriate asset
accounts to ensure proper classification and fair presentation of infrastructure
records in the financial statements; and (iv) fully disclose the total road
network system in the NTFS, including length, value, additions, reductions,
and other movements during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:

a. make a written request from the bank for the copies of debit/credit memo
and exert extra effort to retrieve other supporting documents needed for
other reconciling items and subsequently draw journal entries to record all
valid reconciling items for a fair presentation of the balance of Cash in
Bank-LCCA account in the financial statements; and henceforth,
b. strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2,1996.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor

a. person/s responsible for the issuance of the checks to produce the
disbursement vouchers pertaining to the subject transactions to ascertain
the details and the amount due from debtors in order to prove the accuracy
of the year-end balance of Other Receivable account;
b. concerned official to take appropriate administrative/legal actions against
the officials found responsible for the non-submission or loss of the subject
disbursement vouchers; and
c. Municipal Legal Officer continuously monitor the status of the case filed by
the Municipality in the issue of the irregular check disbursements and
demand settlement or payment for the concerned employee responsible
thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer (MSGO) to prepare and submit
immediately the Acceptance and Inspection Report and other supporting
documents upon receipt and inspection of the items/goods purchased to
the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Voucher (JV) to record the
Inventories in the Municipality’s books and appropriate inventory ledger
cards;
b. MGSO to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting Requisition Issue Slip to the
Municipal Accountant within five days after the end of the month as basis in
drawing a JV to record the issuances;
c. Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory account all
purchases of supplies and materials upon acceptance and inspection of the
delivered items and only recognize the expense upon consumption or
distribution for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement; and
d. concerned municipal official to submit the lacking supporting documents
and a written explanation on the deficiencies noted to substantiate the
disbursement made and facilitate the post audit of the same, Otherwise, the
said transactions shall be suspended in audit.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count of PPE
accounts to prove the existence of the Municipality’s properties, and to
prepare and submit the required report thereon, in proper form within the
prescribed period and subsequently conduct the reconciliation of the
Municipality’s PPE per Inventory report with the records maintained by the
Offices of the Accounting and the GSO. Any discrepancies found should
be investigated, and the necessary adjustment should be made where the
error was committed;
b. require the GSO to update Property Cards for all PPE accounts;
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger
Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land
to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balances for PPE
accounts;
d. submit a request for relief from property accountability and all the required
documents for the property razed by fire to the Audit Team to facilitate
approval thereof, so that the cost of the said property will be dropped from
the books of account; and
e. concerned officials to furnish the Municipal Accountant the necessary
documents needed for the derecognition of the demolished old municipal
building in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the errors in recording the funds
transfer from source agencies amounting to P11,953,277.14; and henceforth,
see to it that the financial transactions of the Municipality are properly recorded
in the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and
other pertinent existing regulations.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor

a. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with
the OlC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net
differences of P213,862.74 and P(271,077.14) between the recorded balances
of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their
reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the
Province;
b. Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required adjusting
journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the
Municipality’s financial statements; and
c. Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of accounts Due
from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the
corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. determine the details of the discrepancies on the balances of CIB-LCCA
accounting and treasury records and subsequently effect the necessary
adjustments, if warranted; and
b. conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in
recording as required under Section 63(F) of the LTOM.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare and submit the liquidation
reports of the fund transfers to DSWD, DOH-IV-A, DAR and COMELEC;
b. Municipal Officials concerned to immediately implement the projects
funded by the DSWD and LGSF-DRRAP; and henceforth,
c. Management to observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in
implementing projects funded by Source Agencies to ensure that funds are
utilized and liquidated on time and the delivery of services and benefits to
the public will be facilitated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer
to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to remit to the BTr/SA all the
unused balance of funds pursuant to the cited regulations, so that the amount
could be immediately used for other projects/programs of the National
Government, and to relieve the Municipality from any accountability/liability for
said unused funds.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor consider the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as
provided under COA Circular No. 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020 to come up
with a reliable PPE balance;
b. Inventory Committee (i) immediately complete the Report on the Physical
Count of PPE and Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network
System and reconcile these reports with the Property Cards, PPE Ledger
Cards, Road Network Ledger Cards and other accounting records; and (ii)
submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team;
c. Municipal Accountant (i) reconcile the year-end balances of the PPE and the
differences of total acquisition costs of PPE account balances reported in the
financial statements and in the PPE schedules; and (ii) recompute depreciation
of local roads and provide for the correct accumulated depreciation of the said
PPE; and
d. Municipal Engineer (i) submit a complete report on the costs, description and
specifications and cost segregation of the road components to the Municipal
Accountant as bases for disclosures in the NTFS; and (ii) regularly assess each
road component for impairment.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor (i) require the concerned barangay officials to immediately
liquidate and/or return the unused balances of funds received, if any; (ii)
regularly monitor and verify the status of implementation of projects funded
from the transfers to barangays; and (iii) ensure that the provisions of COA
Circular No. 94-013 are strictly observed and that all fund transfers to other
LGUs are fully liquidated upon completion of the purpose; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) prepare and submit the schedule of fund transfers
with complete information pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and (ii)
reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers of fund transfers amounting to ₱465,281.23.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer immediately submit the details of reconciling items of ₱554,170.39 (net) and
investigate the variances of ₱111,552,796.00 noted in the Reconciliation Statement of
Actual Amounts.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer immediately reconcile their records on DRRMF in coordination with
the MDRRM Officer to present the correct amounts in the NTFS, SCBAA, SAAOB
and in the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund; b)
Municipal Accountant immediately reconstruct the SLs for all transfers of
unexpended DRRMF to the STF by year of transfer; and c) Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended
DRRMF to the LDRRMF STF bank account.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor, i) thru the MBO and the MDRRM Officer, ensure that
appropriations for DRRM PPAs are realigned and utilized within the DRRM
Fund; and b) Municipal Accountant and the MBO compute the unexpended
balance from the realigned DRRM Fund of ₱9,018,154.48 and include it in the
amount to be transferred to the Special Trust Fund.
b. Municipal Accountant and the MBO review the utilization of the Grants and
Donations under the MOOE account of the MDRRM Office, investigate the
variance of ₱552,500.00 and submit proof of augmentation/realignment if that
amount was found valid and thereafter effect the necessary adjustments to correct
the reported utilization;
c. Municipal Accountant immediately submit the aforementioned paid vouchers
and supporting documents since these were already recognized/recorded in the
books of accounts;
d. Municipal Mayor: i) thru the MDRRM Officer, appropriate funds for the repairs
and maintenance of the DRRM equipment and vehicles under the MDRRM
Office in the General Fund; and ii) thru the Municipal Accountant, MBO and
MDRRM Officer, ensure that the allocation and utilization of DRRM Funds are in
accordance with the guidelines laid down by the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC
No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and
e. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRM Officer and GSO,
submit the lacking Inspection and Acknowledgement Receipt, Requisition Issue
61
Slip and proof of receipt and distribution of the welfare goods to the beneficiaries
and determine the quantity of items that were already distributed/issued and
record the undistributed or remaining food supplies to the appropriate inventory
account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the head of procuring entity, (a)
ensure that the POs are completely and accurately accomplished before its approval
and the complete details regarding the specifications of the goods to be procured are
clearly indicated in the PO/Contract; and (b) thru the BAC and Municipal Accountant,
cause the submission of the authenticated copy of KMIA Medical and Dental Supplies’
License to Operate as medical devices wholesaler/ retailer.

We also recommended that the BAC (a) provide the basis in their computation of the
estimated unit cost per antigen testing kit procured on CY 2021; (b) in every
procurement of drugs and medical devices, require suppliers to submit copies of their
FDA-issued LTO and verify it on the FDA online verification portal; and (c) observe
due diligence in the determination of price estimates of goods prior to commencing
procurement as well as in the evaluation of the legal and technical documents of the
supplier.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Engineer
and the MPDC, submit an explanation on the non-implementation of the project and
ensure that future projects funded by the LGSF are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready and strictly comply with the guidelines related to
implementation of the LGSF projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjustments to reflect the correct CIP and Due to NGAs account balances in the
Trust Fund books and for the immediate transfer of the completed assets to the
General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjustments to reflect the correct Due to NGAs and Trust Liabilities account
balances pertaining to LGSF-AM Construction of Evacuation Facility.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
Municipal Engineer, immediately submit the lacking documents supporting the
payments relative to the implementation of the aforesaid LGSF projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC, Municipal
Engineer and other implementing offices or focal person assigned to the projects and
programs funded by NGAs to act with dispatch on their immediate implementation
to avoid cancellation and possible refund of the funds received by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, (a) thru the Municipal
Accountant, review the Memoranda of Agreement pertaining to the aforesaid
unutilized balances of completed projects and communicate with the source agencies
for the immediate return of such balances; and (b) carefully review the total
utilization of the trust receipts to avoid excessive refund to the source agency and/or
national treasury.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor and the Municipal General Services Officer render a
satisfactory written justification on the continued existence of private
commercial building owned by the GSO, Engr. Confesor on the public market
site as well as the failure of the Management to take action against the use of
government property for private purpose;
b. Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer strictly observe the
requirements on the issuance of business and building permits particularly the
mandatory tenurial instrument in order to stop proliferation of illegally
constructed buildings/structures;
c. Municipal Treasurer and the Market Supervisor immediately submit the (i)
updated list of actual occupants of the said private commercial building in the
public market site; (ii) certified copies of contract of lease or other tenurial
instrument for the building under the name of Engr. Confesor and/or lease
agreements with the occupants/stallholders and their business permits; and
(iii) record of rental fees, business taxes and business permit fees collected
from Engr. Confesor on leasing his commercial building; and
d. Municipal Mayor fast track the LGU’s application to the PRA and compliance
to the requirements laid down by the PRA A.O. Nos. 2019-4 and 2021-01 for
undertaking the reclamation project (Phase 2) and acquiring legal rights/title
over the existing reclaimed land (Phase 1).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the agency head, direct the
concerned department heads to submit written justification on the non-submission of
the information on the actions taken by the MGTP and/or reasons for non-
implementation, otherwise, provide proof of compliance to the audit
recommendations indicated in Finding No. 4 of CY 2017 AAR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) together with the BAC
and the Municipal Engineer, ensure that the implementation and procurement
methods adopted for infrastructure projects and the terms of contracts entered
into by the MGTP are in accordance with the Revised I.R.R. of R.A. No. 9184 and
other related laws, rules and regulations; (b) thru the Municipal Engineer, review
the necessity and validity of the variation orders before approval; (c) thru the
Municipal Accountant, strictly observe the provisions of the Revised I.R.R. on
releasing advance payment for all contracts and ensure the completeness of
supporting documents before making payment; and (d) in view of the delayed
implementation for more than five years, take appropriate actions and avail of the
remedies pursuant to Section 68, Section 8 of Annex E and Section III.A.2, Annex
I of the Revised I.R.R. of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) issue termination of contract to the
erring supplier pursuant to Item III.A.1 of Annex I of the 2016 Revised I.R.R. of
R.A. No. 9184; (b) thru the BAC, consider issuing a Blacklisting Order in
accordance with the Uniform Guidelines For Blacklisting Of
Manufacturers, Suppliers, Distributors, Contractors And Consultants provided in
Appendix 17 of the said I.R.R; and (c) require the concerned officials to render a
written justification on their failure to monitor the supplier’s compliance on the
terms of the contract and failure to recommend its termination despite the lapse
of five years.
91

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer: (a) immediately submit the
latest Statement of Work Accomplished (showing both the material and labor
component) and Statement of Time Elapsed to the Audit Team; and (b) together
with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), monitor and
take necessary actions to address the delay in the project implementation and
ensure that future projects are implemented in accordance with the agreed project
schedule and plans.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) render a
satisfactory explanation/justification on the noted discrepancies in the procurement
of goods intended for donation which are deemed inconsistent with the guidelines of
R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised I.R.R.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the
supporting documents of the goods as part of her internal audit service to the
Municipality.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (MPDC) fast track the implementation of the infrastructure
projects and ensure that all projects are strictly monitored and implemented
in accordance with plans and schedules;
b. MPDC and the Local Development Council establish priorities during
investment programming and include in the Annual Investment Program only
those programs and projects which are feasible, specifically identified and
with resource requirements that are based on duly approved POWs and
Detailed Cost Estimates;
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with
the Municipal Engineer, reconcile their records to determine the correct total
actual cost incurred for each project;
d. Municipal Engineer (i) ensure that the status report of all infrastructure
projects of the municipality is complete and supported with SWA and other
documents required for technical evaluation and inspection; (ii) provide the
completion and inspection report and other documents necessary in the
recording of the projects implemented by the barangays; and (iii) henceforth,
strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
e. Municipal Accountant (i) submit the schedule of the SL balances of the CIP
account as well as the schedule of the related expenditures of completed
projects already transferred to PPE account to facilitate validation of the
reported actual work accomplished per project; and (ii) in coordination with
the Municipal Engineer, submit the aforementioned lacking technical
documents of the projects.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant complete the
reconciliation of their records on Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth and provide
subsidiary schedules for balances totaling ₱16,341.69 to facilitate remittance to the
concerned agencies; b) HRM Officer immediately determine the cause/s of the
discrepancies between the computed contributions and loan amortizations and the
amounts on the billing sent by these agencies and inform the individuals concerned in
order to facilitate the necessary adjustments; and c) Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer ensure that funds collected by the municipal government in
behalf of other government agencies are remitted to them fully and within the
prescribed due dates to avoid incurrence of interests and penalties.

We recommended that the GFPS ensure that the Annual GPB is incorporated
in the Annual Budget, as well as any changes thereto, to ensure gender responsiveness
of GAD programs, projects and activities.

a. Municipal Treasurer to: a.1 follow up her request to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) through the Office of the Municipal Mayor, an authority for the activation and closing of accounts with the PNB pursuant to DOF Department Order No. 01.2017. Likewise, make sure that all municipal funds shall be deposited and maintained with the government depository bank as required under Section 5.2 of Department of Finance (DOF) and Department Circular No. 2017-01; a.2 regularly monitor the outstanding checks and inform the OIC-Municipal Accountant of all stale checks for appropriate adjustment in the accounting records; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to: b.1 ensure that personnel involved in the preparation of the BRS should thoroughly analyze all reconciling items; and b.2 continue to coordinate with the concerned bank for the needed copies of debit/credit memos and see to it that all the items that need adjustments are adjusted immediately in the books of the Municipality to avoid the accumulation thereof and present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: A follow-up letter to the SB had been made by the Municipal Treasurer for the transfer of the inactive account from PNB to the Municipality’s depository bank. The accounts with the PNB had been closed and transferred to Land Bank and Veterans Bank of the Philippines. The Municipal Treasurer monitors the occurrence of the stale checks and coordinates with the owner/handler of the checks, and consequently executes possible action to effect adjustment thereto. The Municipal Accounting Office is regularly coordinating with the bank to furnish the requested copies of debit/credit memos from the concerned bank.
observations.reason: The Authority for the activation and closing of accounts with the PNB was done and the accounts with the PNB was closed and the inactive account transferred from PNB to the Municipality’s depository bank. The Municipal Treasurer monitors the occurrence of stale checks.

observations.recommendation: a. Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count to prove the existence of the Municipality’s property, and to prepare and submit the required report thereon, in proper form. Prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the inventory count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustment should be made where the error was committed; b. GSO and OIC Municipal Accountant to make certain that Property Cards and PPELC are well maintained, complete and updated to facilitate the physical count and reconciliation of inventoried property and accounting records; c. Concerned Municipal Officials to expedite the submission of request for relief from property accountability and the required documents for the destroyed public market to the Commission on Audit to facilitate approval thereof so that the cost be dropped from the books of account; d. Municipal Accountant to provide the correct classification for the Construction in Progress account for completed projects; e. Municipal Accountant to provide the correct depreciation for Work/Zoo Animals and make the necessary adjustment for the said property of the Municipality; and f. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total Road Networks of the Municipality at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical count of PPE and submitted the RPCPPE to the Audit Team but still incomplete. GSO and the OIC Municipal Accountant to make certain that Property Cards and PPELC are well maintained and updated. The GSO is still updating the Property Cards. The Municipal Accountant will manually maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for Land account using excel spreadsheet while waiting for the automation of the ledger cards to be incorporated in the existing computerized system of the Municipality. The Legal Office will endorse to the Commission on Audit (COA) the needed files for the request from property accountability for the property razed by fire at the New Taytay Public Market in year 2010 supported with proper justification on the inability of the Municipality to provide the original copies of some of the supporting documents. As regards to the derecognition of the demolished old municipal building from the books of account, the Municipal Accountant will continue to coordinate with the concerned offices for the required supporting documents as per audit recommendation.
observations.reason: There were PPE items that are not included in the RPCPPE and the items listed in the RPCPPE are not reconciled with the accounting books. Some documents are not available and automation of ledger cards is pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. request for an authority to the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of those payables in the amount of P7,782,392.23 and have been outstanding for more than two years. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents; b. submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and c. record only payables with proper and complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: A request for reversion of Accounts Payable amounting to P7,782,392.23 was granted through Ordinance No. 713 series of 2021. The Municipal Accounting Department assured that recording to Accounts Payable are with complete documentation. An updated schedule is maintained. Valid obligations are forwarded to Treasury Department for payment.
observations.reason: Reversion of the amount of P7,782,392.23 was granted and the Accounting Department maintained updated schedule; valid obligations forwarded for payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the OIC-Provincial Accountant in order to come up with the reconciled amounts of the Inter-Agency reciprocal accounts, Due from and Due to other LGUs. A schedule was prepared to arrive at the reconciled balances and will subsequently record the necessary adjustment in the books. With regards to the reconciling items for unaccounted beginning balance since CYs 2004 and 2016, the Provincial Accounting Office agreed to request for write-off from COA.
observations.reason: Coordination between the Municipal and Provincial Accountants took place and reconciling actions are ongoing; some unaccounted beginning balances require write-off request to COA.

observations.recommendation: a. prepare the necessary JEV adjustment for the errors committed in recording of share from fish pen fees from LLDA and fund transfer from the Province of Rizal amounting to P170,364.30 and P3,000.00, respectively; and b. exert more efforts to locate the documents for the unaccounted balance of Due to NGAs to determine its details. If found futile, request for an authority to the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the unaccounted balance of Due to NGAs of P338,845.33 without documentation which remained outstanding or non-moving for several years.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 017 dated August 2, 2021 was drawn for the reclassification of the P3,000.00 fund transfer from the P3,000.00 fund transfer from the Province. The Municipal Accounting Office opted to retain the amount of P170,364.30 in Due to NGAs account since this is ongoing program reserved for fishing industry. There are no documents available on file that could support the balance of account amounting to P338,845.33. The Municipal Accounting Office will comply with the recommendation for the long outstanding balances.
observations.reason: The fish pen fees share from LLDA amounting to P170,364.30 should be recorded Due to GOCC. The unaccounted balance of P338,845.33 remains in the books and not yet remitted to the BTr/SA.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit whatever balance due to the agencies including long outstanding balances from previous years in accordance with the cited regulations; and b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to make a representation with the concerned agencies to formally inquire whether there were unremitted balances in their records for 2014 and prior years. In case the Municipality has no unremitted balance for the said period, request for a certification of no-arrears; and subsequently request for approval from Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the said Inter-Agency Payables accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were able to remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations. Due to restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, formal inquiries regarding unremitted balances for 2014 and prior years with concerned agencies took place and some of the concerned agencies have been amenable to the process.
observations.reason: Remittances were made; inquiries with concerned agencies regarding long outstanding balances were undertaken though some actions delayed by COVID-19 restrictions.

observations.recommendation: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), thru its Secretariat, to comply with the posting requirements of GPPB Circular No. 2020-01 for transparency of procurements of the Municipality; and b. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements made and to facilitate post audit of the same. Otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The BAC, thru its Secretariat, submitted certification that posting at conspicuous places had been made. The Municipal Accountant submitted two disbursement vouchers and all the supporting documents to the Audit Team and a written explanation on the deficiencies noted.
observations.reason: BAC submitted posting certification; Municipal Accountant submitted disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipality’s representative, the MSWDO representative, and the other concerned officials to henceforth impose strict measures in profiling the beneficiaries particularly in times of calamities by conducting a strict house to house screening in accordance with the requirements and proper coordination with the concerned Barangay Officials, to facilitate the granting of financial assistance to eligible beneficiaries as well as to avoid the return of the cash assistance from the non-eligible recipients; b. MSWDO to submit a written explanation on the deficiency noted on the recipients of SAP, particularly to those beneficiaries who are not registered in the master list but received the cash assistance and those payrolls in handwritten form; and c. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements made to facilitate post audit of the same. Otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO Officer presented an improved process in the implementation of the same program and submitted written explanation on the deficiencies noted on the recipients of SAP. The Municipal Accountant submitted the supporting documents relative to the transactions to the Audit Team to facilitate post audit.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 and ensure proper utilization of the fund; b. BAC, thru its Secretariat, to ensure compliance with the posting requirements of all procurements in accordance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and c. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements made and the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers to facilitate post audit of the same. Otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer adhered with the new guidelines for the 20% Development Fund. The BAC submitted certification that posting at conspicuous places had been made. The Municipal Accountant submitted the disbursement vouchers and the unsubmitted disbursement documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-MPDC and the GAD Focal Person to establish the GAD Database with adequate and systematically gathered GAD information to include gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MPDC and the GAD Focal Person were able to gather data but still need to establish the gathered GAD information into a comprehensive database.
observations.reason: Need to establish the systematically gathered GAD information into a consolidated database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct: a. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements made and the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers to facilitate post audit, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended in audit; b. Concerned Municipal personnel to submit a written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the submitted documents such as Purchase Request, Purchase Order, and Acceptance and Inspection Report; and c. BAC, thru its Secretariat, to ensure compliance with the posting requirement of related information for all procurements of the Municipality in conspicuous places in accordance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the submitted disbursement vouchers and the lacking supporting documents. The concerned personnel submitted written explanations on the deficiencies noted. The BAC submitted certification that posting at conspicuous places had been made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the person/s responsible for the issuance of the checks to produce the DVs pertaining to the subject transactions to ascertain the details and the amount due from debtors in order to prove the accuracy of the year-end balance of Other Receivable account; and direct the Municipal Legal Office to continuously monitor the status of the case filed by the Municipality on the issue of the irregular check disbursements and demand settlement from the erring employees.
observations.managementAction: Presentations of documents or markings were done and pre-trial was undertaken as of this date. Case had been filed against the erring employees and Treasury Department forwarded supporting documents to the Accounting Department for recording.
observations.reason: Case has been filed and pre-trial undertaken; some disbursement vouchers and supporting documents remain unsubmitted or unresolved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
a.1 follow up her request to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) through the Office of
the Municipal Mayor, an authority for the activation and closing of
accounts with the PNB pursuant to DOF Department Order No. 01.2017.
Likewise, make sure that all municipal funds shall be deposited and
maintained with the government depository bank as required under
Section 5.2 of Department of Finance (DOF) Department Circular No. 2017-
01;
a.2 regularly monitor the outstanding checks and inform the OIC-Municipal
Accountant of all stale checks for appropriate adjustment in the
accounting records;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
b.1 ensure that personnel involved in the preparation of the BRS should
thoroughly analyze and account properly all reconciling items; and
b.2 continue to coordinate with the concerned bank for the needed copies of
debit/credit memos and see to it that all the items that need adjustments
are adjusted immediately in the books of the Municipality to avoid
accumulation thereof and present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank
accounts.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count to prove the existence
of the Municipality’s property, prepare and submit to the Auditor the RPCPPE
in the prescribed form. Prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the
inventory count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancies
found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustment should be made
where the error was committed;
b. GSO and OIC Municipal Accountant to make certain that Property Cards and
PPELC are well maintained, complete and updated to facilitate the physical
count of property and equipment and reconciliation of items inventoried as
against the GSO and Accounting records. Likewise, the GSO to issue ARE to
pinpoint the direct person accountable and responsible for the
property/equipment issued. Moreover, renew all ARE every three years.
c. Concerned Municipal Officials to expedite the submission of request for relief
from property accountability and all the required documents for the destroyed
public market to the Commission on Audit to facilitate approval thereof so that
the cost be dropped from the books of accounts;
d. Municipal Accountant to provide the correct classification for the Construction
in Progress account for completed projects;
e. Municipal Accountant to provide the correct depreciation for Work/Zoo Animal
and make the necessary adjustment for the said property of the Municipality; and
f. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total
road network system of the Municipality at year-end.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. request for an authority to the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of those
payables without valid documentation in the amount of P7,782,392.23 and
have been outstanding for more than two years. Thereafter, make the
necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to
Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for
other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents;
b. submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and
properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and
c. record only payables with proper and complete documentation.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the
reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate
adjustments can be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the
Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. prepare the necessary adjustment for the errors committed in recording of
share from fish pen fees from LLDA and fund transfer from the Province of
Rizal amounting to P170,364.30 and R3,000.00, respectively, to present the true
balance of the said accounts. Exercise due professional care in recording the
financial transactions of the Municipality to avoid frequent errors in keeping of
accounts; and
b. exert more efforts to locate the documents for the unaccounted balance of Due
to NGAs to determine its details. If found futile, request for an authority to the
Sangguniang Panlalawigan for the reversion of the unaccounted balance of
Due to NGAs of P338,845.33 without documentation which remained
outstanding or non-moving for several years.

We strongly reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendations that the
Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit
whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of
penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned.
Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan
amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the
cited regulations; and
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to make a representation with the concerned
agencies to formally inquire whether there were unremitted balances in their
records for 2014 and prior years. In case the Municipality has no unremitted
balance for the said period, request for a certification of no-arrears; and
subsequently request for approval from Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion
of the said Inter-Agency Payables accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of
the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare separate reports of
collections for RPT and SET and automatically deposit the proceeds of the additional
1% RPT accruing to the SEF depository account, in compliance with Section 272 of
R.A. 7160 and Section 64(A) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual. We also
recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the correctness of the amount of
RPT and SET collections of to be credited to both RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert efforts in reconciling
the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in all funds, determine
the cause/s of disparity in the balances and record the necessary adjustments in the
books to present the correct balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the:
a. Inventory Committee (i) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and
Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network System and
immediately complete the reconciliation with the Property Cards, PPE Ledger
Cards, Road Network Ledger Cards and other accounting records; and (ii)
submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Engineer (i) submit a complete report on the costs, description and
specifications and cost segregation of the road components to the Municipal
Accountant as bases for disclosures in the NTFS; and (ii) regularly assess each
road component for impairment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer reconcile their respective records on appropriations and obligations, verify
the resulting discrepancies and exercise due care in preparing budget reports to
ensure accurate final budgets and actual amounts in the SCBAA.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer submit the details of reconciling items totaling ₱48,529,178.21 and henceforth
reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting
discrepancies and submit the required budget reports and reconciliation statement
within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers, obligation requests, journal entry vouchers and supporting
documents for the subject transactions since these transactions were already
recognized in the books; and henceforth, ensure strict adherence to the provisions of
Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on
the timely submission of accounts to the Auditor.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer immediately reconcile their records on DRRMF in coordination with
the MDRRM Officer; b) the Municipal Accountant immediately (i) reconstruct the
SLs for all transfers of unexpended DRRMF to the STF by year of transfer; (ii)
record necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Trust
Liabilities-DRRMF account and to present the correct amounts in the NTFS and in
the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund; and (iii)
henceforth, record the DRRM funds received from other sources to the Trust
Liabilities-DRRMF account; and iv) the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended DRRMF to the LDRRMF
STF bank account.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRM Officer, upon recommendation of the MDRRM Council, i) facilitate
the preparation of the revised/supplemental investment plan for newly
identified PPAs to be funded by the reprogramming from special trust fund
(STF) and from DRRMF in the general fund (GF); ii) ensure that the
supplemental investment plan for PPAs funded by STF is approved by the
sanggunian and the realignment/reprogramming funded by the current 5%
and continuing appropriations of DRRMF in the GF is covered by a
supplemental budget duly enacted by the sanggunian; and submit explanation
on the variance of 6,846 sacks of rice between MDRRMO report and per
audit. For proper monitoring and reporting purposes, the PPAs funded by
STF should be identified separately from the PPAs funded by the current year
5% DRRMF in the GF; and.
b. Municipal Accountant i) thru the BAC and other concerned offices, submit the
lacking documents discussed in Table 8.4 as well as the rice and other welfare
goods distribution list; ii) submit the DVs enumerated in Table 8.5; and iii)
thoroughly review the disbursements, as part of the internal audit system of
the Municipality to determine the completeness and propriety of supporting
documents before certifying the DVs.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant i) ensure that all receipts and utilization of funds under a
special account are properly recorded in the SAGF; ii) thru the BAC and other
concerned offices, submit the lacking documents discussed in Table 9.3; and iii)
thoroughly review the disbursements, as part of the internal audit system of the
Municipality to determine completeness and propriety of supporting documents
before certifying the DVs;
b. Municipal Mayor (i) require the Local Finance Committee to prepare the
monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the
prescribed format (Annex C) pursuant to Item 4 of DBM LBC No. 125; and (ii)
thru the BAC Secretariat, ensure that the complete and detailed item description is
included in the Purchase Order, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 96-010; and
c. BAC, i) thru the BAC Secretariat, create an account in the GPPB online portal
using the name of the Municipality of Taytay as “Procuring Entity” so that the
uploaded NOA, POs, APP and other information would be properly reported
under the name of the Municipality of Taytay; and ii) continue to comply with the
posting requirements of GPPB Circular 2020-001 for information purposes to
promote transparency and accountability in the government transactions.

We recommended that the:
a. LCE, through the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC), (i) reevaluate the number of
infrastructure projects which were identified to be undertaken “by
administration” and assess whether the Municipality’s limited manpower and
equipment as well as the technical capability to undertake is sufficient to allow
project implementation and completion within the time schedule; and (ii) fast
track the implementation of the infrastructure projects and make sure that all
projects are strictly monitored and implemented in accordance with plans and
schedules;
b. MPDC and the Local Development Council establish priorities during
investment programming and include in the Annual Investment Program only
those programs and projects which are feasible, specifically identified and
with resource requirements that are based on duly approved POWs and
Detailed Cost Estimates;
c. Municipal Engineer ensure that the status report of all infrastructure projects
of the municipality is complete and supported with SWA and other documents
required for technical evaluation and inspection;
d. Municipal Accountant (i) review the SLs of the CIP account and make sure
that each project will only have one SL; (ii) submit the schedule of the SL
balances of the CIP account as well as the schedule of the related expenditures
of completed projects already transferred to PPE account to facilitate
validation of the reported actual work accomplished per project; and (iii) use
the proper accounts and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct
CIP and Advances to Contractors account balances in the Trust Fund; and
e. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer (i) submit the technical
documents enumerated in Table 10.3 and 10.4; and (ii) maintain a complete
set of technical documents (refer to Table 10.3 for projects implemented by
“administration”) per project for easy monitoring of the project
implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, i) thru the Municipal
Accountant, review the Memoranda of Agreement pertaining to the aforesaid
unutilized balances of completed projects and communicate with the source agencies
for the immediate return of such balances; and ii) thru the MPDC, coordinate with
the implementing offices of the abovementioned on-going and unimplemented
projects so that appropriate actions may be undertaken thereon for effective and
efficient utilization of the funds.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor i) instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the
Improvement of the Slaughter House to avoid cancellation of the project; ii)
thru the Local Finance Committee (LFC), ensure strict compliance with the
quarterly reporting and posting of the fund utilization and status
program/project implementation as required by the guidelines of LGSF-FA to
LGUs; and iii) thru the Municipal Agriculturist, secure the required
certificate of registration from the Bureau of Animal Industry as required by
R.A. No. 8485 or the “Animal Welfare Act of 1988”;
b. Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct
CIP and Due to NGAs account balances in the Trust Fund; and
c. BAC and other concerned offices properly plan the procurement of the goods,
services and infrastructure projects to achieve timely implementation and/or
fulfillment of the purpose of fund received by the Municipality from the
national government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), (i) thru the
Municipal Accountant and HRM Officer, discontinue charging personal travels
against the funds of the Municipality and instead, require them to submit application
for leave of absence; (ii) enjoin all department heads to properly plan/schedule their
activities in the barangays to minimize the transportation costs incurred by the
Municipality particularly the rentals of motorized bancas; and (iii) thru the
Municipal Accountant and immediate supervisors of the employees claiming
pumpboat rentals, strictly implement the standard rates established by Ordinance
No. 311, s.2018.
For the non-availability of a regular transportation in the island barangays,
the LCE and the concerned department heads were also encouraged to strategize and
utilize the municipal funds to address the problem of sea transportation such as
budget proposal for the purchase of additional watercrafts in the ensuing year, which
can ferry officials and employees from the official station to their destination and
vice-versa, as well as those barangay constituents who cannot afford to hire pumpboat
services for their travel to mainland Taytay.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant reconcile the records on
Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth and provide subsidiary schedules for balances
totalling ₱20,910.40 to facilitate remittance to the concerned agencies; b) HRM
Officer immediately determine the cause/s of the discrepancies between the computed
contributions and loan amortizations and the amounts on the billing sent by these
agencies and inform the individuals concerned in order to facilitate the necessary
adjustments; and c) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that
funds collected by the municipal government in behalf of other government agencies
are remitted to them fully and within the prescribed due dates to avoid incurrence of
interests and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MPDC, with the assistance of the GFPS, to establish a complete and updated
GAD database, and conduct gender analysis to serve as a basis for addressing
gender issues and concerns in GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing
years; and
b. Local Sanggunian to formulate an updated GAD Code or ordinances that will
support the Municipality’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling
women’s human rights, economic empowerment and gender-responsive
governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s
empowerment.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor require the full settlement of
the disallowance; and (b) Municipal Accountant transmit the proof of settlement of
ND to the Audit Team for issuance of Notice of Settlement of
Suspension/Disallowance/Charge (NSSDC).

d. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all paid disbursement painstakingly submitted to the Accounting Office for recording so that the true/correct balance of the accounts for the period are properly presented in the financial statements and to avoid the loss or misplacement of the vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Compliance to the recommendation is being observed.

OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned. observations.recommendation: a. properly plan and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under 20% DF; b. ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20% DF have gone through a thorough study and priority ranking; and c. continue to invest in high-impact or developmental projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: OIC-MPD Officer gave assurance of full compliance to the recommendations. OIC-MPD Officer gave assurance of compliance. Management still included expenditure which do not partake the nature of investment/capital.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive LDRRMFIP including the list of projects and activities to be funded out of the previous year’s unexpended LDRRMF to ensure Municipality’s capacity to respond and recover from the impacts of disasters and emergencies that may occur; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Audit Team in the period prescribed in the regulations.

observations.recommendation: a. direct the OIC-MDRRMO to ensure that identified priority PPAs are implemented as planned to better prepare for and lessen the impact of natural and man-made hazards/disasters and promote socio-economic growth in the Municipality; and b. review the identified projects, programs and activities in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for DRRM and evaluate them with priorities, needs and desired outcome and make amendments to the AIP as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MDRRMO, though new in her office, gave assurance through written communication to the Auditor of full compliance to the recommendations. The OIC-MDRRMO ensures an Annual Investment Plan aligned with the needs of the locality and its constituents.

observations.recommendation: a. ensure that all GAD PPAs are properly planned and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan; b. strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the GAD objectives are fully attained and the actual expenditures will not exceed the approved budget per PPA; and c. submit justification on the overspending of P7,159,414.31.
