observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. make the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the above-cited liabilities to its proper account;
b. refrain from recording the unliquidated cash advances as Accounts Payable and record the expenses only when liquidation reports were submitted; and
c. ensure that all liabilities of the Municipality are correctly recognized in the books of accounts pursuant to the above-cited Circular.

We reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances by sending demand letters regularly, particularly to those AOs who are active in service, and in case of failure to liquidate the same despite such demand, withhold the amount of any money due them pursuant to the afore-cited Circular;
b. require the AOs to liquidate their CAs within the prescribed period; and
c. refrain from granting additional cash advance unless the previous ones are already liquidated or settled.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the above subject accounts, and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations; and
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to speed up and prioritize the determination of the details of the unaccounted balances amounting to P1,067,091.47 in coordination with the other concerned government agencies so that appropriate disposition on said balances can be undertaken.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Cards, prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible at the beginning of every year; and b) the Municipal Accountant reconcile the recorded Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts with the Treasury report on the unpaid taxes for the current year as well the uncollected taxes in prior years and effect the necessary adjustments, as appropriate.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee complete the actual physical count of all supplies and materials and prepare the report thereon, reconcile the results of the count with available records in the Accounting and General Services Offices and submit the RPCI to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the GSO (a) present to the Auditor the copies of TCTs and Deed of Sale/Donation establishing ownership in favor of the muicipality for verification and audit; (b) conduct inventory of all its real properties whether acquired, donated or reclaimed; (c) institute proceedings for the issuance of tax declarations and TCTs in the name of the municipal government in coordination with the Municipal Assessor; and (d) regularly inspect the real properties owned by the municipal government to keep track of their condition and to safeguard them  from squatters, unlawful occupants or the like.

We recommended that the:
Inventory Committee (i) immediately complete the physical inventory of all municipal property; (ii) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network System to be reconciled with the Property Cards, PPE Ledger Cards, Road Network Ledger Cards and other accounting records; and (iii) submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team; and
Municipal Engineer (i) submit a complete report on the costs, description and specifications and cost segregation of the road components to the Municipal Accountant as bases for disclosures in the NTFS; and (ii) regularly assess each road component for impairment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer submit the details of reconciling items totaling ₱62,509,838.94 and henceforth reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting discrepancies and submit the required budget reports and reconciliation statement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, obligation requests, journal entry vouchers, copies of accountable forms issued and supporting documents for the subject financial transactions since these were already recognized in the books of accounts; and (b) henceforth, ensure strict adherence to the provisions of Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the prompt rendition of accounts.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor issue an order to all officials and employees concerned to refund the excessive and extravagant payment of travel expenses totaling ₱99,849.26 and ₱291,710.00, respectively; and
Municipal Accountant, henceforth, enforce strict adherence to EO No. 77 and DILG MC No. 2019-82 in order to prevent the incurrence of excessive and extravagant expenditures as defined under Sections 5.1 and 6.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the:
Local Chief Executive:
re-evaluate, thru Human Resource Management Officer, the existing contracts of services that need to be converted to contractual or casual appointment and ensure that the contracts or appointments contain the position titles and salary grade allocation patterned to the Compensation and Position Classification System and DBM Budget Circular No. 2018-4 for uniformity and consistency of salary rates;
strictly comply with the provisions of Section 133 of 2017 ORAOHRA that no individual COS employees shall be made to perform functions pertaining to regular positions; and
ensure that JO agreements specifically state the duties and responsibilities of each contracting party and the expected output or work to be performed
Municipal Accountant review the completeness and propriety of supporting documents attached to the payrolls/DVs covering payment of salaries/wages of COS/JO personnel to ensure compliance with pertinent rules and regulations and the regularity of their claims before processing them for payment.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the assistance to the Barangays are paid thru issuance of check under the name of the Barangay; and duly supported with official receipts; and.
Municipal Accountant cause the submission of the official receipts and related MOA and henceforth, review the completeness and propriety of supporting documents before processing claims for payment.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Mayor require the full settlement of the disallowance; and b) the Municipal Accountant transmit the proof of settlement of ND to the Audit Team for issuance of NSSDC and henceforth, ensure that all NDs covered with NFD are promptly recorded in the books of accounts.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count to prove the existence of the Municipality’s properties, prepare and submit to the Auditor the RPCPPE in the prescribed form. Prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the inventory count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustment should be made where the error was committed; b. GSO and OIC Municipal Accountant to make certain that Property Cards and PPELC are well maintained, complete and updated to facilitate the physical count of property and equipment and reconciliation of items inventoried as against the GSO and Accounting records. Likewise, the GSO to issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to pinpoint the direct person accountable and responsible for the property/equipment issued. Moreover, renew all ARE every three years; c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record back in the books of accounts the cost of the public market of P17,902,908.91; and secure approval from the Commission on Audit before dropping the same in the books of accounts; d. concerned Municipal Official to submit request for relief from property accountability together with all the required documents for destroyed public market to the Commission on Audit, to facilitate approval thereof so that the cost can be dropped from the books of accounts; e. Municipal Engineer to expedite the submission of the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects of the Municipalities; f. GSO to maintain Local Road Inventory and keep a complete LRNPC for all roads and its components; and g. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system of the Municipality at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee had conducted a complete physical inventory of all the PPE including the Road Network and submitted the report to the Audit Team. The Inventory Committee had submitted the Plan. The system provider has been relayed to the system provider. Updating the Property Cards by the GSO. The Municipal Accountant is maintaining its PPE records in excel and is reconciled with the Accounting records. The Municipal Engineer submitted the list of road network of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The inventory undertaken was incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor the outstanding checks and inform the Municipal Accountant of all stale checks for appropriate adjustment in the accounting records; b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to: b.1 ensure that personnel involved in the preparation of the BRS analyzed the accounts and all the reconciling Items; and b.2 continue to coordinate with the concerned bank for the needed copies of debit/credit memos and see to it that all the items that need adjustments are adjusted immediately in the books of the Municipality to avoid accumulation thereof and present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: According to the LBP Taytay branch manager, request letter was forwarded to LBP Head Office, but still no reply as of this date. Personnel assigned in the preparation of the BRS analyzed the accounts and all the reconciling items. Current reconciling items are effected in the books except for outdated items which supporting documents can no longer be located.
observations.reason: There are outstanding checks that became stale and the OIC- Accountant was not informed, thus proper adjustment cannot be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a) strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances by sending demand letters regularly, particularly to those AOs who are active in service, and in case of failure to liquidate the same despite such demand, withhold the amount of any money due them pursuant to the afore-cited Circular; b) require the AOs to liquidate their CAs within the prescribed period; and c) refrain from granting additional cash advance unless the previous ones are already liquidated or settled.
observations.managementAction: Often reminded of consequences for non-compliance pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Scrupulously implemented. Certification as to Liquidation of previous Cash Advance is required prior to grant of a new one. 99 percent of the outstanding cash advance as of December 31, 2019 had been liquidated on the first quarter of CY 2020. Accounting Office is continuously coordinating with Treasury Dept and reminding them of five days limitation on liquidation.
observations.reason: There are outstanding cash advances and additional cash advance was granted although previous cash advance was not fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a) link the Real Property Tax Assessment System (RPTAS) with the GAAMS; b) Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate with the Municipal Assessor regarding RPT and SET for restricted properties; and b) make necessary adjustments to reflect realistic amount of RPT and SET that should be collected during the year.
observations.managementAction: Linking of Real Property Tax Assessment System (RPTAS) with the GAAMS System (GAAMS) was requested from the system provider. The amount of delinquent RPT is no longer considered in setting up the current year’s collectibles since it has already been set-up in the previous years. Deduction of uncollectible RPT and SET for restricted properties is being done to reflect the realistic amount that should be collected during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the person/s responsible for the issuance of the checks to produce the DVs pertaining to the subject transactions to ascertain the details and the amount due from debtors in order to prove the accuracy of the year-end balance of the Other Receivables account; and b) direct the Municipal Legal Office to continuously monitor the status of case filed on the issue of the irregular check disbursements and demand settlement or payment from the concerned employee/s responsible thereof.
observations.managementAction: Case had been filed against the erring officials and employees. Presentations of documents or markings were done and pre-trial was undertaken as of this date. Documents supporting the disbursement voucher check encashment were forwarded to the Accounting Office for recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a) exert effort to retrieve available records/documents in order to ascertain the details of the documents; b) immediately prepare adjusting entries to record the prior years’ repair and maintenance expenses to its proper expense accounts; c) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the status of the prior years’ incomplete projects totaling P1,035,535.67; d) prepare adjusting journal entry to record the recoupment of mobilization fee for the construction of Taytay Public Market amounting to P25,395,734.70 by debiting CIP - Building and Other Structures account and crediting Advances to Contractor account; and e) regularly review and analyze the above-cited accounts in order to reflect the correct balances at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Extra effort was available exerted to retrieve the documents but to no avail. Adjusting entries to record prior years’ repair and maintenance were already taken up under GJ-100-19-05-101 dated May 31, 2019 and GJ-200-19-06-019 (SEF). The recoupment of mobilization was recorded by debiting CIP-Building and crediting Advances to Contractor per JEVs. Coordination with the Municipal Engineer was made and documents are being retrieved.
observations.reason: Further examination of the records is ongoing; some documents could not be retrieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to immediately revert the journal entries made to record the unliquidated cash advances for different purposes amounting to P16,530,320.25 and ensure only valid claims for the delivered goods or services on account in the normal course of trade and business operation of the Municipality be recognized as AP pursuant to the above-cited Circular.
observations.managementAction: Reverted the unliquidated cash advances for the amount P8,597,982.16 amounting to accounts which lapsed for the three years claiming period per JEV# 100-19-07-148 dated July 31, 2019. Ensure only valid claims are recognized as AP pursuant to the Circular.
observations.reason: The rest of the unliquidated cash advances remain under review; some continue to be carried as obligations pending validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the differences of these accounts and prepare the necessary journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with the Provincial Government was made. Subsequent remittances and adjustments were made and reconciled balances were established. Prepare the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the above subject accounts, and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations; and b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to speed up and prioritize the determination of the details of the unaccounted balances amounting to P1,067,091.47 in coordination with the other concerned government agencies so that appropriate disposition on said balances can be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Remittance made by the Municipal Treasury depend on the schedules prepared by the Accounting Office. Complete remittances were subsequently effected. An Accounting staff has been designated to maintain and monitor movement of these liability accounts from all funds to assure accuracy. Coordination with concerned agencies ongoing.
observations.reason: Remittance schedules and full validation of long-outstanding items are still ongoing; some dependencies on accounting schedules delayed full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Supplemental Budget for CY 2019 the payment for the differential of Municipal Health Officials with SG 20 and above totaling P492,433.20.
observations.managementAction: This was finally paid on December 20, 2019 through Chk/DJ JEV No. 100-10-12-5279.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to: a) submit within the prescribed period all their accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the aforementioned regulations, to facilitate timely review/post-audit of disbursements; and b) coordinate with the system provider and closely monitor the upgrading of their computerized system to avoid delay in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office submitted the accounts on time. Coordinated with the system provider regarding early notice for any upgrading in order to avoid delay in the future.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Officer to: a) properly plan and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under 20% DF; b) ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20% Development Fund have gone through careful planning and study and priority ranking; and c) continue to invest in high-impact or developmental projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: Out of the seven (7) identified projects, two (2) were implemented, other two (2) were realigned to another projects and the last three (3) were not implemented; correspondences and re-planning were undertaken by MPDO. The OIC-MPDO gave assurance of compliance.
observations.reason: There were inevitable predicaments along the way causing partial implementation; re-alignment and realignment of projects occurred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of the above-cited projects to immediately address all issues that may arise during its implementation and to avoid additional delays in the completion of the same; and b) require the Municipal Engineer and the OIC-MPDO to properly identify and prioritize the projects to be submitted to DILG for funding under LGSF-AM in the future to ensure the timely implementation of the said projects for the benefits of the general public.
observations.managementAction: The five (5) identified projects were 100% completed as of Dec. 20, 2019. Corresponding excess cash were returned to the Bureau of Treasury. MPDO and Municipal Engineer coordinated and submitted project reports to DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) the former BuB Focal Person to properly turnover all the related documents pertaining to the implementation of the different projects to the LPRAO Head; b) LPRAO-Head to coordinate with the former BuB Focal Person to conduct another monitoring and prepare a report thereon to properly account all the procured equipment and ensure that the same are used for its intended purpose; and c) LPRAT to conduct quarterly monitoring and reporting pursuant to the above-cited Joint Memorandum Circular to ensure the attainment of the objective and sustainability of the projects funded from fund transfer from National Government Agencies under BuB.
observations.managementAction: The former BUB Focal Person has left without formal turnover of the different projects to the office; documents remain intact in the office. Regular monitoring and reporting are ongoing and reports are prepared for DILG validation.
observations.reason: Initial coordination and monitoring were undertaken but no formal turnover was completed by the former BuB focal person; further monitoring needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Municipal Legal Officer to review the terms of the contract, and if warranted, impose upon the contractor proper sanctions as stipulated in the contract; b) Municipal Engineer take appropriate actions to immediately address the issue behind the delayed completion of the NTPM to enable the Municipality and its constituents to attain full benefits of the project; and c) Municipal Engineer to review and reassess the reasons and causes of its failure to timely implement the NTPM project to avoid similar situation in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer reviewed and reassessed the reasons and causes of failure to implement the NTPM project. The delayed opening was due to upgrading of electrical and installation of meter. At present the Market is fully operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) OIC-MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive LDRRMFIP integrating the prior years’ unexpended balances of LDRRMF; and furnish the OCD and the DILG-LGOO a copy of the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002; and b) Municipal Accountant to ensure figures and information are correctly stated in the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MDRRMO stated she assumed position in November 2019 and was not the one who prepared the LDRRMFIP for CY 2019. She prepared a comprehensive LDRRMFIP for CY 2020. The Municipal Accountant cited delays due to generation and finalization of monthly Trial Balance but promised to comply.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP for CY 2019 was submitted late (May 2, 2019) contrary to stipulated April 30 submission date; reconciliation of figures between reports and notes remains ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to prepare and submit to the Audit Team a duly certified Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, and b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the figures and information are correctly stated in the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with the concerned department; regular monitoring ongoing. Municipal Accountant asserted misconception on classification of items and will correct disclosures. Monthly reports submission was delayed due to issues in generation and finalization of Trial Balance but are being worked on.
observations.reason: Late submission of monthly reports and reconciliation issues caused delays in full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO Head to ensure that qualified personnel immediately undergo training on the proper operation of the procured Drone Camera to operate the same as the need arises.
observations.managementAction: The training was headed by a hired professional trainer. MDRRMO personnel underwent training on operation of drone and related equipment. Regular operations planned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to: a) ensure that all GAD PPAs are properly planned and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; b) strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the GAD objectives are fully attained and the actual expenditures will not exceed the approved budget per PPA; and c) submit justification on the overspending of P7,159,414.31.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer assured proper GAD planning and budgeting and implementation of calendar activities; management stated no overspending and that discrepancy arose from erroneous reporting of utilization for CY 2019 GPB. They will observe proper processes and coordinate with DILG as needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) direct the MSWMB to integrate the solid waste management plans and strategies of the Barangays in the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to help them establish fully functional MRFs; and b) require the LNB President to actively participate in the MSWMB meetings so barangays are properly represented and ensure the barangay solid waste management plan aligns with the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: A continuous interaction through meetings and discussions with the barangays is being implemented. Partnership of MGAD and LCPC in all LCPC's PAPs is being coordinated. Regular monitoring being conducted and results submitted to DILG for validation.
observations.reason: Some barangays have not yet fully adopted the required plans; coordination and monitoring are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the a) Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Card, prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the total amount due and collectible in the ensuing years; and b) Municipal Accountant set up the Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts for CY 2019 and onwards based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer showing the total amount of tax due and collectible.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted to the Municipal Accountant the partial Certified List of Taxpayers showing the total amount due and collectible, however, this was only submitted in June 2019.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, determine the cause of disparity in balances and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already made a reconciliation between these accounts and the necessary adjusting entries and fund transfers were already made.
observations.reason: There is still a disparity of ₱1,192.08 between the balances of the said accounts as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a) the Market Committee, through the Market Supervisor, ensure that lease of market stalls are covered by lease contracts stipulating the stall reference numbers, area of stall being leased, amount and effectivity of lease and strictly enforce the terms and provisions of the Market Code; b) the Municipal Treasurer review the computation of market fees to determine under- assessment/under-collection, immediately demand payment from stallholders, submit proof of collection and deposit thereof to the Audit Team and henceforth collect market fees as stipulated in the Market Code; and c) the License Inspector conduct annual inspection of business establishments to ensure collection of business permit fees in compliance with the provisions of Sections 2 and 29, Article A, Chapter III of the Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The Municpal Treasurer submitted the supporting documents and schedule of stallholders who have already settled their unpaid balances totalling ₱31,917.50 with their corresponding proof of payments. Also, notices had been sent to all identified stallholders and the Municipal Mayor reconstituted the Market Committee.
observations.reason: The Management did not submit AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the municipal government, through the Municipal Mayor, to 1) immediately secure the required water permits from the NWRB and submit authenticated copies thereof to the Auditor; 2) submit the plans, specifications and implementation schedules of the twenty-three water supply systems to the NWRB for approval and secure duly approved copies thereof to be used as reference in project monitoring and in NWRB, MDFO and COA-Technical Audit Specialist (TAS) project inspection; 3) immediately request for NWRB inspection of the completed water supply systems and obtain Certificates of Compliance therefor; 4) review and submit the water tariffs proposal to the NWRB for approval; and 5) to immediately request for the necessary amendment of the SPLA, identify the Level II and III water systems in the amended SPLA and submit for reference in audit authenticated copies of the a) amended SPLA; b) Program of Works used as basis for the cost allocation in Annex 2 of the SPLA dated September 8, 2016; c) BSP Monetary Board opinion on the monetary and balance of payments implications of subject domestic borrowing of the municipal government in compliance with Section 123 of RA No. 7653 and BSP Circular No.769 dated September 26, 2012; d) certification on the debt service and borrowing capacity of the municipal government obtained from the Department of Finance – Bureau of Local Government Finance (DOF-BLGF); and e) Executive Order establishing the Subproject Implementation Unit in accordance with Section 5.2(d)(1) of the SPLA.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Bids and Awards Committee, the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator 1) re-evaluate the economic, financial and technical viability of the Taytay Municipal Wide Water System Development Project; 2) review the implementation and procurement methods adopted for the project and the terms of contracts entered in relation thereto; and 3) inquire on the legal implications and advisability of availing the remedy of contract termination for convenience as provided under Section III.B of Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2004 dated December 22, 2004 should this proved to be in the best interest of the municipal government.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer 1) re-evaluate the procurement method adopted and the PGP’s qualification as a servicing agency; 2) review the terms of the Agency-to-Agency Agreement for necessary amendments; and 3) furnish the Auditor copies of the aforementioned documents necessary for evaluation of compliance of the conditions set forth in Section 5 of Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 18-2007 dated May 31, 2007 as amended. 

Further, we recommended for the immediate submission of 1) the printout copies of postings in the PhilGEPS and certification from the Head of the BAC Secretariat on posting at conspicuous place in the municipal government’s premises of the subject Notices of Award, Notice to Proceed and Contract; and 2) the required technical and detailed engineering documents and of an accounting of the remaining materials amounting to P115,163,465.30 which should have been fully delivered by Atlanta Industries Inc. as of March 14, 2017. Henceforth, we recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books of accounts and prepare separate financial statements for the water supply system and other duly created public utilities and economic enterprises of the municipal government. The budget for public utilities and economic enterprises should likewise be presented separately in the Annual Budget in accordance with the provisions of DBM LBC No. 112 dated June 10, 2016. We further recommended that the guidelines for the setting up and operation of public utilities and local economic enterprises now contained in DBM LBC No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 be strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted to the Audit Team copies of the Program of Works and Detailed Estimates and Bill of Materials, plans and drawings and requests for and the status  of the water, drilling and/or conditional permits of the twenty-three water supply systems.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Bids and Awards Committee, the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator 1) re-evaluate the economic, financial and technical viability of the Taytay Municipal Wide Water System Development Project; 2) review the implementation and procurement methods adopted for the project and the terms of contracts entered in relation thereto; and 3) inquire on the legal implications and advisability of availing the remedy of contract termination for convenience as provided under Section III.B of Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2004 dated December 22, 2004 should this proved to be in the best interest of the municipal government.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer 1) re-evaluate the procurement method adopted and the PGP’s qualification as a servicing agency; 2) review the terms of the Agency-to-Agency Agreement for necessary amendments; and 3) furnish the Auditor copies of the aforementioned documents necessary for evaluation of compliance of the conditions set forth in Section 5 of Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 18-2007 dated May 31, 2007 as amended.
Further, we recommended for the immediate submission of 1) the printout copies of postings in the PhilGEPS and certification from the Head of the BAC Secretariat on posting at conspicuous place in the municipal government’s premises of the subject Notices of Award, Notice to Proceed and Contract; and 2) the required technical and detailed engineering documents and of an accounting of the remaining materials amounting to P115,163,465.30 which should have been fully delivered by Atlanta Industries Inc. as of March 14, 2017. Henceforth, we recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.
We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books of accounts and prepare separate financial statements for the water supply system and other duly created public utilities and economic enterprises of the municipal government. The budget for public utilities and economic enterprises should likewise be presented separately in the Annual Budget in accordance with the provisions of DBM LBC No. 112 dated June 10, 2016. We further recommended that the guidelines for the setting up and operation of public utilities and local economic enterprises now contained in DBM LBC No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 be strictly observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the a) Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property
Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Card, prepare and submit to the
Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the total amount due
and collectible in the ensuing years; and b) Municipal Accountant set up the Real
Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real
Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts for CY
2019 and onwards based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer
showing the total amount of tax due and collectible.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, determine the cause of disparity in
balances and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct
account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that a) the Inventory Committee complete the actual
physical inventory, reconcile the results of the count with available records in the
Accounting and General Services Offices and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the
Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
b) the concerned Accounting and GSO personnel reconstruct the property cards
and ledgers based on source documents or vouchers to facilitate reconciliation of
physical inventory report and book records.

We recommended that the GSO a) conduct inventory of all real properties of
the municipal government whether acquired, donated or reclaimed; b) institute
proceedings for issuance of tax declarations and certificates of title in the name of
the municipal government in coordination with the Municipal Assessor; and c)
regularly inspect the real properties owned by the municipal government to keep
track of their condition.

We recommended that the concerned municipal government officials adopt
austerity measures and exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds
taking into consideration the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated
October 29, 2012 and that the BAC ensure strict compliance with the provisions of
RA No. 9184 and its IRR to avoid disallowances in audit and possible administrative
and criminal charges.

We recommended that the concerned Accounting Office personnel
reconstruct the maintenance/repair record of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or
operated by the municipal government.

We recommended that 1) the Municipal Accountant record the necessary
adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF account; and 2) the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO cause the
immediate transfer of the unexpended amount of P15,863,106.15 to the separate
depository account for DRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer submit the details of reconciling items totaling P5,337,913.65 and henceforth
reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting
discrepancies and submit the required budget reports and reconciliation statement
within the prescribed period.

We recommended that a) the Market Committee, through the Market
Supervisor, ensure that lease of market stalls are covered by lease contracts
stipulating the stall reference numbers, area of stall being leased, amount and
effectivity of lease and strictly enforce the terms and provisions of the Market Code;
b) the Municipal Treasurer review the computation of market fees to determine
under-assessment/under-collection, immediately demand payment from
stallholders, submit proof of collection and deposit thereof to the Audit Team and
henceforth collect market fees as stipulated in the Market Code; and c) the License
Inspector conduct annual inspection of business establishments to ensure collection
of business permit fees in compliance with the provisions of Sections 2 and 29,
Article A, Chapter III of the Revenue Code.

We recommended that the TPWSMO prioritize setting up a utility billing
software and updating its records to determine the correct amount of receivables
from water bills, institute actions to collect unpaid bills and surcharges from
customers and submit a report thereon to the Audit Team. Henceforth, we
recommended that the TPWSMO secure printouts and data backups regularly and
see to it that the provisions of SB Ordinance No. 245 series of 2016 are complied
with.

We recommended that the concerned municipal government officials a)
institute legal proceedings for the removal of the illegally constructed structures and
for the collection of taxes and revenues due the municipal government; and b)
apprise the Audit Team of the actions taken by each office on subject structures and
businesses from the start of construction and business operation up to present.
Filing of appropriate administrative and criminal charges against municipal
government officials who failed to perform their duties and thus responsible for the
use of government properties for private purposes and proliferation of illegally
constructed structures in the municipal government properties is likewise
recommended.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers of Construction in
Progress and Due from LGUs accounts, reconcile the total cost incurred per
project with the records of the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with
the Municipal Engineer and immediately submit the subsidiary ledgers,
vouchers and supporting documents of completed projects for audit;
b. Municipal Engineer submit the POW, Detailed Cost Estimates, approved
plans/drawings, technical specifications, SWA, PERT/CPM network diagrams,
as-built plans for completed projects and photographs of infrastructure projects
with appropriations totaling P256,567,992.58 and the POW and Detailed Cost
Estimates of infrastructure projects forming part of the unimplemented
programs and projects with total appropriations of P86,421,774.57;
c. Municipal Mayor, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC)
and the Municipal Engineer evaluate the programs and projects with remaining
appropriations as of December 31, 2018, recommend to the Local Development
Council reprogramming of those which the municipal government will no longer
be able to implement due to causes not attributable to the fault or negligence of
the municipal government and its officials, determine unexpended balances of
completed projects and recommend to the Local Finance Committee re-
appropriation of said balances to fund other priority programs and projects of
the municipal government;
d. MPDC and the Local Development Council establish priorities during
investment programming and include in the Annual Investment Program only
those programs and projects which are feasible, specifically identified and with
resource requirements that are based on duly approved POWs and Detailed
Cost Estimates; and
e. Municipal Mayor, as the one primarily responsible for all government funds and
property of the municipal government, ensure that the required reports and
documents are submitted by the concerned municipal government officials to the
Audit Team in such form and within the period prescribed by the Commission
pursuant to the provisions of Sections 39 and 122 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Local Finance Committee and
the Municipal Accountant see to it that the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No.
2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 are strictly complied with to ensure that the 20%
Development Fund is utilized solely for the implementation of development projects
that partake the nature of investment and capital expenditure.

We recommended that the a) BAC and the end-user unit ensure strict

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that a) funds be appropriated for the survey and titling of
land; b) the Municipal General Services Officer be instructed to inventory all real
properties of the municipal government whether acquired, donated or reclaimed and
to institute proceedings for issuance of tax declarations and certificates of title in the
name of the municipal government in coordination with the Municipal Assessor; and
c) the Municipal General Services Officer be instructed to regularly inspect the real
properties owned by the municipal government to keep track of their condition.

We recommended that the inventory team be required to conduct actual
physical inventory, reconcile the results of the count with available property and
accounting records and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of
the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and
book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.

We recommended that the accounting and general services office be required
to reconstruct the property cards and ledgers based on source documents or vouchers
to facilitate reconciliation of physical and book inventories.

We recommended that funds be appropriated for payment of insurance
premiums and that a staff in the general services office be assigned to conduct
inventory of all insurable properties and to monitor expiration and renewal of
insurance policies.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer be required to
locate/obtain Certificates of Registration of subject vehicles costing P14,358,612.17
and that a staff in the general services office be assigned to monitor and process
vehicle registrations.

We recommended for the accounting office to reconstruct the
maintenance/repair record of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the
municipal government.

We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-
GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended for the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to
prioritize the preparation of the 10-year solid waste management plan, including the
closure plan for the existing controlled dump, for approval of the National Solid
Waste Management Commission. We have also recommended that funds be
appropriated for improvement of the municipal government’s solid waste management
system, completion of sanitary landfill, establishment of MRF in every barangay or clusters
of barangay and public education and information activities on ecological waste
management.

We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Sections 335 and 336
of RA No. 7160, Amended Rules and Regulations of EO No. 180, CSC MC No. 33,
5.1997 and RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the lacking documents.
Henceforth, we recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No.
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012.

We recommended for the HOPE to take cognizance of the aforecited
provisions in the GCC in deciding whether requests for time extensions are based on
meritorious grounds or not.

We recommended for the accounting office to immediately reconstruct the
subsidiary ledgers for the trust fund accounts and submit a copy thereof to the
Auditor together with copies of Memorandum of Agreement with source agencies
and its supporting Work and Financial Plan or Program of Works.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the subject bank
reconciliation statements.

We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No.
2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No.
2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Creates an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory of PPE
and prepare and submit a report thereon to the Auditor;

b) Directs OIC-GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the
inventory report with their respective records in order investigate and
make necessary adjustments where the error was committed. Assign
additional personnel exclusively for the reconciliation of the GSO and
Accounting records to augment the manpower requirement of the said
Offices and in order to expedite the completion of the mentioned activity;

Instructs the OIC-GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain a
complete and updated Property Cards and Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively and issue ARE to pinpoint
accountability based on the reconciled inventory report of PPE; and

Expedite the submission of all the required documents to support your
request for relief from property accountability for the destroyed public
market to the Commission on Audit to facilitate approval thereof so that
the cost be dropped from the books of accounts.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instructs the OIC-GSO to
immediately facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of insurable
PPE so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied
with the GSIS under the General Insurance Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough
review and analysis of the Property, Plant and Equipment account particularly
those pertaining to 2012 and prior years' acquisitions. Continue to trace back
prior years® transactions in order to establish the details of the accounts so that the
depreciation for the same could be correctly computed and make the
necessary corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts for the inaccurate
computation of depreciation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a)

b)

€)

d)

€)

Update, prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation
Statements together with all the required attachments within the
prescribed period to the Auditor for review and verification to detect
immediately frauds/irregularities in the use of cash resources;

Ensure that personnel involve in the preparation of the BRS should
thoroughly analyze and account properly all reconciling items;

Conduct periodic reconciliation of balances of bank accounts between
accounting and treasury records;

Request for the upgrade of the accounting system software to ensure
the accuracy of the subsidiary ledger balance for each bank account; and

Continue to coordinate with the concerned bank for the needed copies
of debit/credit memos and see to it that all the items that need
adjustments are adjusted immediately in the books of the Municipal
Government to avoid accumulation thereof and present the correct
balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a)  Directs the Municipal Accountant to use the duly certified list of

taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and amount due and
collectible from each of them as provided by the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer at the start of each year to serve as basis in
setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax
Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS
for LGUs, Volume I;

b) Requires the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis
of the RPT/SET Receivables accounts and prepare an adjusting
entry in order to reflect the correct balances of the said Receivable
accounts as at yearend;

¢) Instructs the concerned officials to immediately coordinate with
each other concerning the uncollectible RPT and total delinquent
RPT collectibles;

d)  Allocates fund for the possible upgrading of the existing software of

the Accounting Office in order to generate Subsidiary Ledger per

taxpayer; and

e) Instructs the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the
RPT/SET Receivable accounts in order to reflect in the correct
balance in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1. Requires the person/s responsible for the issuance of the checks, to
immediately submit/produce the Disbursement Vouchers pertaining to
the subject transactions to prove the correctness of the yearend balance of Other Receivable account;

2. Directs the Municipal Accountant to disclose the nature and details of
the Other Receivables account in the Notes to Financial Statements;

3. Take appropriate administrative/legal action against the official/s
found responsible for the non-submission or loss of the subject
disbursement vouchers; and

4. Instructs the concerned officials to make a follow-up with the Office
of the Ombudsman as regards the status of case filed by the
Municipality on the issue of the irregular check disbursements and

demand settlement of payment from the concerned employee/s
responsible thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Continue to exert extra effort to trace back prior years’ records and
documents which gave rise to the accounts payable;

b) Submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid
and properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate
payment;

¢) Recorded payables without valid documentations and/or which have
been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual
claim has been filed should be reverted with the approval of the
Sanggunian Bayan to Government Equity;

d) Make the necessary adjusting entry on the Accounts Payable and
Insurance Expense accounts to record the payable that was already
paid during the year: and

e) Record only payables with proper and complete documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Regularly make a detailed review of the accounts and
investigate/determine the causes of the unreconciled amount; and

b) Exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial
transactions so that the financial statements would not be misleading.

We recommended that the Municipal officials concerned immediately
submit to the Auditor all the required documents relative to the utilization of the
fund transfers from DILG and DOLE to the Municipal Government in order for
the audit team to ascertain the propriety and validity of the transactions and to
avoid audit suspension for lack thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Requires the Bids and Awards Committee to send out written
invitations to COA and at least two (2) other observers at least
three calendar days before the date of the procurement stage/activity
to be witnessed; and

b) Instructs the BAC Secretariat to be compliant in posting
procurement/bidding notices in the PhilGEPS and in the
Municipality’s website as required by R.A. 9184 to attain the widest
circulation of the agency’s procurement/bidding activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Directs the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
discharge his functions efficiently, study the projects under 20%
Development Fund, closely monitor the implementation of those
ongoing ones, determine the causes of the non-implementation of the
seven projects listed under it and render informative monitoring
report thereon;

Ensures that implementation of development projects is kept on track
for optimum utilization of the funds allocated therefor; and

Continues to invest in high-impact or developmental projects for
inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office continues to
remit the monthly GSIS contributions and loan amortizations withheld from
employees in full and within the required period to avoid penalties, such as
interests and surcharges that maybe imposed by the GSIS, and the possible non-
approval of loans or payments of benefits of retiring personnel.
